info.title: Flapjack API
info.description: Drop-in replacement for Algolia search. Provides full-text search with filters, facets, geo-search, and more.

post.summary: List all indexes available in an Algolia application.
post.description:
Validates that `appId` and `apiKey` are non-empty, then calls the Algolia
`/1/indexes` endpoint and returns index name, entry count, and last-updated
timestamp for each index. Returns 400 if credentials are missing or 502 if
the upstream Algolia call fails.

get.summary: GET /1/configs — list all Query Suggestions configurations

delete.summary: DELETE /1/configs/:indexName — delete configuration (does NOT delete the suggestions index)

200.description: Custom entry counts grouped by language

400.description: Invalid dictionary name or batch payload
Invalid dictionary name or search payload

post.summary: POST /1/events - Algolia Insights API compatible event ingestion

get.summary: GET /1/events/debug - Return recent events from the debug ring buffer

parameters.name: hitsPerPage
parameters.description: Number of indices per page (default 100, max 1000)

get.summary: Search an index using query-string parameters on GET /1/indexes/{indexName}

200.description: Search results with hits and facets

post.summary: Add or update documents in batch
Browse all documents in an index with pagination

post.description: Validate that the target index exists and is in NeuralSearch mode, resolve the AI provider configuration, perform keyword retrieval for RAG context, and return either a JSON response or an SSE stream depending on the `Accept` header and `stream` flag.

200.description: Chat answer (JSON) or SSE stream when stream=true

schema.description: Server-Sent Events stream of chat chunks

404.description: Index not found or not in neural search mode

post.summary: Clear all documents from an index
Delete objects matching a filter query

get.summary: Exports an index as a compressed snapshot file returned as a binary download response.

post.summary: Search for facet values with optional filtering
Get multiple objects by ID in batch
Execute multiple search queries in a single request, optionally merging results via federation.

requestBody.description: Batch search request with multiple queries

post.summary: Search an index with full-text query and filters

requestBody.description: Search parameters including query, filters, facets, and pagination

200.description: Search results with hits and facets

post.summary: Restores an index from an S3 snapshot, downloading and installing the snapshot bytes into the local index directory.
Uploads an index snapshot to the configured S3 bucket, returning the snapshot key on success.

get.summary: List available S3 snapshots for an index
Get task status for a specific index
Get a single object by ID

parameters.name: objectID
parameters.description: Object ID to update or create

parameters.name: createIfNotExists
parameters.description: Create the record if it doesn't exist (default: true)

post.summary: Generate a secured API key with restrictions

get.summary: Get an API key by value

post.summary: One-click migration from Algolia to Flapjack.
post.description:
Fetches settings, synonyms, rules, and all objects from the source Algolia
index and imports them into the target Flapjack index in a single call.

get.summary: Get all configured source CIDR allowlist entries.

put.summary: Replace the entire source CIDR allowlist.

post.summary: Append or update a single source CIDR entry.

delete.summary: Delete a source CIDR allowlist entry.

get.description:
Returns usage statistics aggregated across all indexes.
`:statistic` may be a comma-separated list of statistic names.

Returns usage statistics filtered to a single index.
`:statistic` may be a comma-separated list of statistic names.

delete.summary: DELETE /1/usertokens/{userToken} - GDPR deletion for all insight events tied to a user token
delete.description:
Multi-store cleanup: purges insight events from analytics collector AND
deletes the personalization profile cache for the user token. Ordering is
deterministic (analytics first, then profile cache) with best-effort
semantics — each store is cleaned independently so a failure in one does
not block cleanup of the other.

get.summary: List experiments with optional index prefix/suffix filtering and pagination.
get.description:
Supports Algolia-compatible query parameters for filtering by index name pattern.
Results are sorted by creation time with deterministic tie-breaking on numeric ID.

# Returns

A paginated response containing the matching experiments, page count, and total count.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of experiments to return

parameters.name: indexPrefix
parameters.description: Filter experiments by index name prefix

parameters.name: indexSuffix
parameters.description: Filter experiments by index name suffix

post.summary: Estimate A/B test sample size and duration for a given minimum detectable effect and traffic split.
post.description:
# Arguments

* `State(state)` - Application state containing the optional analytics engine
* `Json(body)` - A/B test request with variants and configuration including minimum detectable effect size

# Returns

An HTTP response containing either an error response or an AlgoliaEstimateResponse with calculated sample sizes per variant and estimated duration in days.

Returns an error if: variants count is not exactly 2, MDE size is outside (0, 1), or traffic percentages are not each in [1, 99] and do not sum to 100.

# Behavior

Calculates required sample size using a simplified formula with 95% confidence interval and 80% statistical power. Queries the analytics engine for historical daily search traffic (past 30 days) to estimate experiment duration. If traffic data is unavailable, uses a conservative default duration.

get.summary: Fetch a single experiment by numeric ID or UUID, hydrated with live metrics.
get.description:
Retrieves the experiment from the store, converts it to the Algolia DTO format,
and overlays click/conversion/search metrics from the analytics engine when available.

# Returns

The Algolia-format AB test payload with metric fields populated, or an error response.

put.summary: Replace an experiment's mutable configuration fields while preserving its identity and lifecycle state.
put.description:
Merges the request body with the existing experiment, keeping `id`, `status`,
timestamps, and conclusion unchanged. Falls back to existing values for optional
fields not provided in the request.

# Returns

The updated experiment in Algolia DTO format, or an error response.

409.description: Experiment is in an invalid status for update

delete.summary: Delete an experiment by numeric ID or UUID.
delete.description:
Removes the experiment from the persistent store. This operation is irreversible.

# Returns

JSON action response confirming deletion with the experiment's numeric ID and index name,
or an error if the experiment is not found.

409.description: Experiment is in an invalid status for conclude

get.summary: Compute and return experiment results with statistical analysis.
get.description:
Fetches experiment metadata and analytics metrics from control and variant indices, computes frequentist z-test significance, Bayesian probability, sample ratio mismatch detection, and CUPED variance reduction when applicable, then returns a comprehensive results response.

# Arguments
- `state`: Application state containing experiment store and analytics engine
- `id`: Experiment identifier (UUID or numeric ID)

# Returns
HTTP JSON response containing experiment metrics, statistical analysis, gate status, and recommendation. Returns error response if experiment store unavailable or experiment not found.

post.summary: Start a previously created experiment, beginning traffic assignment.
post.description:
Resolves the experiment by numeric or UUID path parameter, then transitions
its status to running via the store.

# Returns

JSON action response with the experiment's numeric ID and index name,
or an error if the experiment is not found or not in a startable state.

409.description: Experiment is in an invalid status for start

post.summary: Stop a running experiment, freezing its traffic assignment.
post.description:
Resolves the experiment by numeric or UUID path parameter, then transitions
its status to stopped via the store.

# Returns

JSON action response with the experiment's numeric ID and index name,
or an error if the experiment is not found or not in a stoppable state.

409.description: Experiment is in an invalid status for stop

post.summary: POST /2/analytics/cleanup - Remove analytics data for indexes that no longer exist (local only)

delete.summary: DELETE /2/analytics/clear - Clear all analytics data for an index (local only)

post.summary: POST /2/analytics/flush - Flush buffered analytics events to disk immediately (local only)
POST /2/analytics/seed - Seed demo analytics data for an index (local only, no fan-out)

200.description: Average click position and daily breakdown

get.summary: GET /2/conversions/revenue - Revenue from purchase conversions, grouped by currency

200.description: Revenue by currency with daily breakdown

get.summary: GET /2/countries - Search counts grouped by country code
GET /2/filters/:attribute - Top values for a filter attribute

200.description: Top values for a filter attribute

get.summary: GET /2/geo - Geographic breakdown from country field
GET /2/geo/:country - Top searches for a specific country
GET /2/geo/:country/regions - Region (state) breakdown for a country
GET /2/overview - Server-wide analytics overview across all indices
GET /2/searches - Top searches ranked by frequency
GET /2/searches/count - Total search count with daily breakdown
GET /2/searches/noClickRate - No-click rate with daily breakdown
GET /2/searches/noResultRate - No-results rate with daily breakdown
GET /2/status - Analytics status (local only, no fan-out)

200.description: Unique user count and daily breakdown

AddDocumentsResponse.description:
Response variants for document ingestion endpoints.

- `Algolia` — single-index batch with a scalar `taskID`.
- `MultiIndexAlgolia` — multi-index batch with per-index `taskID` map.
- `Legacy` — Meilisearch-style response with `task_uid` and `status`.

AlgoliaAbTest.description: Top-level A/B test object returned by GET /2/abtests/{id} and inside list responses.

AlgoliaConfiguration.description: Configuration object for an A/B test.

AlgoliaFilterEffects.description: Filter effects metadata (outlier + empty-search exclusion stats).

AlgoliaVariant.description: A single variant in an A/B test.

AnalyticsRateDateEntry.description: Analytics time-series entry pairing a date with a rate value.

AnalyticsRateWithDatesResponse.description: Analytics response containing an aggregate rate and per-date breakdown.

AnalyticsTopSearchEntry.description: Analytics response entry for a top search query with its count.

BatchRequest.description: A single operation in a batch request body.

BatchSearchRequest.description: Batch search request supporting legacy parallel queries plus federated merge options.

conversationId.description: Client-supplied conversation ID for multi-turn continuation.

ConcludedExperimentResponse.description:
Concrete conclude-response schema.

The generic `Experiment` model allows `conclusion` to be absent for draft and
running states, but a successful conclude response always includes it. Keeping
this as a separate DTO lets the OpenAPI contract enforce that stronger promise.

CreateKeyRequest.description:
Deserialize an incoming API key creation request with Algolia-compatible camelCase field names.

All fields except `acl` are optional and fall back to sensible defaults (empty strings, zero, or empty vecs) when absent.

DictionaryName.description: The three dictionary types supported by the API.

DictionarySettings.description:
Per-tenant dictionary settings.

The only field is `disableStandardEntries`, a nested map:
`{ "stopwords": { "en": true }, "plurals": { "fr": true }, ... }`

propertyNames.description: The three dictionary types supported by the API.

Experiment.description:
A/B test experiment linking a control arm to a single variant arm on a given index.

Supports two mutually exclusive variant modes:
- **Mode A** — query-time parameter overrides (`queryOverrides`) applied to the same index.
- **Mode B** — a completely separate index (`indexName`) for the variant, optionally with team-debiased interleaving.

All JSON (de)serialization uses camelCase field names.

FederatedHit.description:
A federated hit preserves arbitrary document fields while making `_federation`
metadata explicit in both runtime values and the published schema.

FederatedResponse.description: Flat federated batch response returned when the top-level `federation` field is present.

FederationConfig.description: Top-level federated batch settings shared by request parsing and OpenAPI.

FederationMeta.description: `_federation` metadata attached to every hit in a federated batch response.

HybridSearchParams.description:
Parameters for hybrid (keyword + vector) search.

Meilisearch-style: `"hybrid": {"semanticRatio": 0.8, "embedder": "mymodel"}`
Algolia-style: synthesized internally when `mode: "neuralSearch"`.

InsightEvent.description: Sent by client via Insights API (click, conversion, view events).

KeyApiResponse.description:
Algolia-compatible API response DTO for key GET/list endpoints.
Never exposes internal fields (hash, salt, hmac_key).

LanguageDictionaryCounts.description: Per-language custom entry counts for the languages endpoint.

ListIndexItem.description:
Single index entry returned by the list-indices endpoint.

Field names are serialized in camelCase to match the Algolia REST API wire format.
`replicas`, `primary`, and `virtual_replica` are omitted from the response when `None`.

MigrateFromAlgoliaRequest.description:
Request payload for migrating an index from Algolia to Flapjack.

Contains Algolia credentials and the source index name. When `target_index` is
omitted the source index name is reused. Set `overwrite` to replace an existing
target index; otherwise a 409 Conflict is returned.

overwrite.description:
If true, delete any existing target index before migrating.
Without this, migrating to an existing index returns 409.

QsMutationResponse.description: Standard success response for query-suggestions mutation operations.

QueryOverrides.description:
Query-time parameters overridable per variant arm (Mode A).
All fields optional — only set what differs from the main index settings.

RecommendRequest.description: A single recommendation request within a batch.

ResultsResponse.description:
Full statistical results payload for an experiment.

Contains arm-level metrics, sample-size gating status, frequentist significance,
Bayesian probability, SRM detection, guard-rail alerts, CUPED adjustment status,
interleaving analysis, and an actionable recommendation string.

SearchRequest.description:
Algolia-compatible search request supporting both JSON body fields and URL-encoded `params`.

Covers keyword search, facet lookups, geo filtering, analytics flags, hybrid
search controls, and federated batch-only options such as per-query weights.

decompoundQuery.description:
Whether to split compound words in Germanic languages (de, nl, fi, da, sv, no).
Default: true (matching Algolia behavior). Set to false to disable.

facet.description: Facet name for type=facet multi-search queries

facetQuery.description: Facet query string for type=facet multi-search queries

maxFacetHits.description: Max facet hits for type=facet multi-search queries

params.description: URL-encoded params string (used by multi-query). Merged during deserialization.

SearchResponse.description: Algolia-compatible search response returned by query endpoints.

SetSettingsRequest.description:
Deserializable payload for the `POST /1/indexes/{indexName}/settings` endpoint.

All fields are optional; only supplied fields are merged into the existing `IndexSettings`.
Unrecognized keys are captured in `other` via `#[serde(flatten)]` and reported as unsupported.

tags.name: query-suggestions
tags.description: Query suggestions configuration and build endpoints
